impacted_domains_still_load_bearing: YES — 'advisory scope' materially undersells it. meta.Domains is consumed by THREE gates plus two non-gate sinks, all by literal name: (1) checkADRCoverage (plan.go:516-533) iterates over impactedDomains and requires each be covered by a cited Accepted ADR — this IS the per-domain iteration the spec cites; (2) checkADRCitations (plan.go:480-482) intersects each cited ADR's Domains against impactedDomains to emit adr-cite-irrelevant; (3) divergence.go:142 attribution (the only one R1 actually rewrites); plus contextpack/budgeter.go:180 (context-pack domain block) and domain/show.go:85. R1 changes ONLY the divergence attribution; ValidatePlan still passes the raw impacted list into checkADRCitations (plan.go:146) and checkADRCoverage (plan.go:156) unchanged. So the field is decidedly NOT vestigial — but the spec's 'advisory' framing hides that the SAME file-path entries that R1 fixes in the divergence lane remain live, name-matched inputs to two plan-time gates R1 never touches.
scope_creep_guard_lost: YES, and unacknowledged. Today candidateDomains := meta.Domains means attribution is SCOPED to declared domains: a file edited in a domain the spec never declared attributes to "" and fires adr-divergence-unowned. That is an accidental but real blast-radius / scope-creep signal — 'this bead touched a domain outside the spec's declared scope.' R1's glob-across-ALL-domains makes any file owned by any manifest pass silently regardless of whether the spec declared that domain. The spec frames this purely as eliminating false positives and never names the lost negative-direction check. Recommendation (the key design tension, surfaced explicitly): PRESERVE it as a WARN — when a changed file attributes (by glob) to a domain NOT in the spec's declared Impacted Domains, emit an advisory 'file attributed to undeclared domain <d>; add it to ## Impacted Domains or split the bead' rather than dropping the guard entirely. This keeps the blast-radius contract as advisory drift detection while still fixing the spurious-unowned error.
resolve_vs_validate: validate-at-approve is the more ZFC-coherent ROOT fix and is currently MISSING; resolve-by-glob is a necessary but PARTIAL patch. Reading the bug as 'the author wrote file paths where ADR-0032 mandates domain dir-name identifiers' is at least as defensible as 'resolution should be glob-based' — ParseSpec (spec.go:62-85) silently stores 'genevieve/review.py' as a bare lowercased pseudo-domain that no OWNERSHIP dir and no ADR Domains field can ever match. Resolve-by-glob fixes the divergence lane but leaves the malformed list flowing UNCHANGED into checkADRCoverage (spurious adr-coverage-missing: no ADR declares 'genevieve/review.py') and checkADRCitations (spurious adr-cite-irrelevant: empty intersection). So the spec's L274 claim that R1 makes the file-path case pass 'CONSISTENTLY between the divergence gate and the plan adr-coverage gate' is not supported by the In-Scope set — plan.go's iteration over impacted is untouched. A validate/normalize-at-approve step (reject or normalize a file-path Impacted-Domains entry to its owning domain at spec-approve, with a clear error) fixes the root malformation for ALL THREE consumers at once and enforces ADR-0032's identifier contract. The two are COMPLEMENTARY: glob-resolution makes attribution robust to omitted domains; approve-time validation keeps the declared list well-formed so the coverage/cite gates stay coherent. The spec should at minimum acknowledge that R1 does not make the plan-time coverage/cite gates tolerate file-path entries, and either bring those into scope or add validate-at-approve.
adr_tension: REAL and unresolved — needs an ADR-0032 amendment or an Open Question. ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 is explicit and emphatic: 'Domain identifier is the OWNERSHIP.yaml directory name. All three artifacts (spec.md ## Impacted Domains, OWNERSHIP.yaml location, ADR Domains field) MUST use the same short-tag identifier set ... Rejected alternatives: path-like identifiers (ambiguous — internal/foo vs foo).' Consequence (−) reinforces: 'Existing repos must update spec impacted-domains to use canonical short tags.' R1 DEMOTES Impacted Domains to 'advisory scope' and TOLERATES file-path entries — directly contradicting ADR-0032's identifier mandate and its explicit rejection of path-like identifiers — while the spec cites ADR-0032 (L88-92) as merely the mechanism R1 'operates inside,' never flagging the contradiction. Meanwhile the spec leans on ADR-0036 (L81-87, L259-265) as the 'direct application' justifying glob-across-ALL-domains, but ADR-0036 is about LOADER FALLBACK REMOVAL (don't synthesize internal/<domain>/** from a domain NAME); it says nothing about WHICH domain SET attribution consults. Globbing the file against every domain rather than the declared subset is a NEW decision, not an application of ADR-0036 — the spec over-claims ADR-0036 cover. Net: an ADR-0032-vs-this-spec tension on the identifier contract that the spec must resolve (amend ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 to say Impacted Domains is advisory and ownership is resolved by glob, OR record it as an Open Question), not paper over by pointing at ADR-0036.
blocking: Unacknowledged dropped guard: R1 silently removes the 'file edited in an undeclared domain' signal (declared-scope-bound attribution). Spec must either acknowledge-and-accept this with rationale, or preserve it as a WARN (file glob-attributes to a domain not in ## Impacted Domains). Add to Requirements/Non-Goals explicitly.

ADR-0032 contradiction unresolved: R1 demotes Impacted Domains to advisory and tolerates path-like entries, contradicting ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 (identifier = OWNERSHIP dir name; path-like REJECTED). Resolve via an ADR-0032 amendment or an Open Question; do not cite ADR-0036 (loader-fallback scope) as cover for a decision it does not make.

Incomplete-fix coherence gap: R1 fixes divergence.go attribution but leaves checkADRCoverage (plan.go:516) and checkADRCitations (plan.go:480) iterating the SAME literal file-path Impacted Domains, so a file-path-Impacted-Domains spec still fails plan validation with spurious adr-coverage-missing / adr-cite-irrelevant. Either bring the plan-time coverage/cite resolution into R1's scope, or add a validate/normalize-at-approve requirement, or scope-document the residual. The L274 'consistent between divergence and plan adr-coverage gate' claim is currently unsupported.
summary: CHANGES. The new semantics ('## Impacted Domains is advisory scope; ownership resolves by glob across ALL domains') is internally INCOMPLETE and has an unresolved ADR-vs-ADR tension, not merely a philosophical quibble. Three substantive, code-grounded issues: (1) R1 silently drops a real guard — declared-scope-bound attribution today surfaces 'file touched an undeclared domain' as unowned; after R1 any owned file passes silently regardless of declared scope; the spec never names this and should preserve it as a WARN. (2) R1 directly contradicts ADR-0032 sub-decision 1, which MANDATES Impacted Domains use OWNERSHIP dir-name identifiers and EXPLICITLY REJECTS path-like identifiers; the spec cites ADR-0036 (loader-fallback removal, which is silent on which domain SET to consult) as cover for a decision ADR-0036 does not make, and never flags the ADR-0032 conflict — an amendment or Open Question is required. (3) The fix is incomplete: meta.Domains stays load-bearing in checkADRCoverage and checkADRCitations, which R1 leaves iterating the literal file-path entries, so the same malformed input still produces spurious adr-coverage-missing / adr-cite-irrelevant at plan time — making the spec's 'consistent between divergence and adr-coverage gate' claim unsupported and arguing that validate/normalize-at-approve (rejecting path-like Impacted Domains, per ADR-0032) is the more ZFC-coherent root fix, complementary to glob-resolution. None of these are blockers to the OTHER three beads (R2/R3/R4), which are clean; they are scoped to R1's contract semantics.
